one_time_description: Settlement income - legal expenses recognized in Administration and Governance segment
economic_notes: Nation has investments in Six Nations Ventures LP (16.67%), Blueberry River Enterprises LP (100%), 0851799 BC Ltd o/a Creever Trading Post (100%), Bluevest Contractors Inc (50%), Blueberry Economic Development Inc (100%). Restricted cash of $65.18M received from Province of BC under Interim Agreement for land restoration and protection. Subsequent event: $252M settlement with Government of Canada signed August 2022; separate BC settlement funds to be paid to newly established BRFN Restoration Society which will operate independently of BRFN.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2022 but audit report is dated December 20, 2024 — very late filing. Large restricted cash item of $65.18M from BC Provincial Interim Agreement for land restoration deposited to lawyer trust account, matched by deferred revenue of $65.18M (Restoration and Protection Fund). Significant one-time settlement income of $4,622,758 in legal expenses. Subsequent event: $252M federal settlement signed August 2022 (not reflected in these statements). ISC Treaty Land Entitlement debt of $4,416,748 also on balance sheet. Three GBEs accounted for under modified equity method. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($5,413,727) + Other government funding ($453,264) + First Nation Health Authority ($954,653) = $6,821,644. OSR includes industry funding, elders/monitors, OGC revenue, BC Hydro, trust income, donations, other revenue net of carry forwards. Distributions and interest to Nation members of $5,142,000 is the largest single expense item in Administration and Governance segment.
warnings: One-time items ($1) represent 0.0% of revenue — surplus may not reflect ongoing operations
